name: Admissions SJT Synthetic Demographic Analogs Are High-stakes Stress Tests, Not Fairness Validation
statement: In the adjacent high-stakes admissions context, Walsh et al. use synthetic demographic-injected analogs as an early fairness stress test for open-response SJT automated scoring, but the design supports diagnostic sensitivity probing rather than subgroup fairness validation or operational decision evidence.
population_or_context: Open-response Casper SJT automated scoring for admissions or selection.
time_horizon: Full text retrieved and extracted on 2026-06-02.
quantitative_note: The source reports a paired original-versus-synthetic demographic fairness pilot; this finding emphasizes the bounded diagnostic role.
caveats: Adjacent SJT admissions responses are not ordinary classroom student answers.
The fairness experiment is described as an early pilot and has small-sample limits.
evaluation_method: Paired comparison of automated LLM evaluations on original responses and analogous demographic-injected synthetic responses.
limitations: Does not replace real subgroup fairness validation.
Does not establish deployment readiness in high-stakes admissions scoring.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases the synthetic demographic-analog fairness pilot and its sample-size caveat.
